mascotLines: I used to just fetch the ball. Now apparently I can fetch it AND put it away for you — kind of like the new feature where firms manage and charge a client’s saved payment method themselves. I’m very good at chores now.

A squirrel darted past while I was testing the Pending Activation print job. It got away, but the print job didn’t — every row finally prints now, past row 35 and everything. I have priorities, and I stand by them.

I finally learned to bury multiple treats and remember exactly where all of them are. Turns out that’s basically what the new scheduled bulk client-chat messages do — set them all up once, and they go out right on time without anyone lifting a paw.
Long chat threads used to slow everything down, like dragging a leash through mud. Now big threads send fast — no mud, no drag, just zoomies.

items.text: Manage and charge payment methods on behalf of the client
items.desc: Firms can now add, remove, and see the expiration status of a client's saved payment methods — and charge invoices using them directly, instead of relying on the client to keep a valid method on file.

items.text: Document timestamps in the Client portal
items.desc: Clients now see the exact time a document was uploaded today (and just the date for older ones), making it easier to spot the most recent file after a rename.

items.text: Fix auto-move for Completed jobs
items.desc: Before, jobs marked “Completed” before their final pipeline stage would get stuck there. Now they keep auto-advancing through the remaining internal stages like they should.

items.text: Update Job Status automation card — design polish
items.desc: The “Update Job Status” automation card just got a visual refresh to match the current design system. No functional changes.

items.text: Automatic invoicing on subscription purchases and upgrades
items.desc: When a firm buys seats, adds seats, or upgrades their plan, TaxDome now automatically calculates the price and generates the invoice — no manual billing steps needed on our end.

items.text: Scheduled bulk client-chat messages
items.desc: Firms can schedule one message to go out to many clients at once, manage everything from a new “Scheduled” section, and send now, reschedule, delete, or reassign in bulk. Clients are only notified when the message actually goes out, not when it's scheduled.

items.text: CRM import improvements: preview and results screens
items.desc: Contact and account imports now show a preview screen before you commit, plus a results screen showing what was created, updated, or skipped — with a downloadable CSV of any skipped rows.

items.text: CRM import improvements: clearer errors and warnings
items.desc: Import now explains the real reason a row was skipped (like a bad email or an unrecognized team member) instead of a generic error, and auto-fixes what it safely can rather than dropping the row.

items.text: Stripe reconciliation was breaking payments on healthy accounts
items.desc: A reconciliation bug was incorrectly flagging healthy connected Stripe accounts as unhealthy, causing payments to fail. Fixed.

items.text: Prepayments stayed enabled after disabling a payment provider
items.desc: Turning off a payment provider didn't turn off the prepayments option tied to it, which could confuse clients trying to pay. Now it does.

items.text: Recurring invoices could double-activate under concurrent sign requests
items.desc: Signing a recurring invoice twice in quick succession could cause both requests to activate it. Fixed so only one goes through.

items.text: Recurring invoice templates crashed on an invalid assignee type
items.desc: Creating a recurring invoice template with certain assignee setups caused a crash instead of a clear error message. Fixed.

items.text: QuickBooks Online: “Deposit To” account settings
items.desc: Fixed how online vs. offline payment deposit accounts are configured for the QBO integration.

items.text: Documents stuck in “Sending” status after a delivery failure
items.desc: If the background job delivering a document failed, the document could get permanently stuck showing “Sending.” Fixed.

items.text: Uploading a document to a job silently failed in some cases
items.desc: Switching between jobs while uploading could cause a document to silently fail to attach. Fixed.

items.text: Download was enabled on collapsed document folders
items.desc: Trying to download a folder without expanding it first threw a confusing backend error. Now it's handled properly.

items.text: Images disappeared from organizer templates
items.desc: Images embedded via the template editor could vanish due to a cleanup bug. Fixed.

items.text: Client login failed after portal access was re-granted
items.desc: Clients who were re-granted portal access after losing it could get stuck with a false “incorrect code” error during email login. Fixed.

items.text: Password updates were failing on all clusters
items.desc: A backend error was blocking password changes entirely. Fixed.

items.text: Firm members without “Assign team members” permission couldn't create client accounts
items.desc: An overly strict permission check was blocking account creation for team members who should have been allowed. Fixed.

items.text: Client account creation showed a false “name already taken” error
items.desc: Adding a contact during new client-account creation could trigger a false duplicate-name error, even though the account was actually created successfully. Fixed.

items.text: Client-user batch updates could crash on removal
items.desc: Removing a stale or missing link during a batch update of client users could crash instead of completing gracefully. Fixed.

items.text: Account role updates could fail under concurrent requests
items.desc: Updating firm member account roles at the same time as another change could hit a database error. Fixed.

items.text: Client chat messages were slow to send on large threads
items.desc: Sending a message in a long-running chat thread was re-processing the entire thread every time, causing multi-second delays. It's much faster now.

items.text: Cloning a pipeline duplicated automation templates instead of reusing them
items.desc: Cloning a pipeline was creating brand-new copies of every linked automation template instead of pointing back to the originals. Fixed (this was a regression).

items.text: Bulk-moving jobs between stages could throw an error
items.desc: Certain bulk stage-move actions hit an unexpected error when parameters were missing. Fixed.

items.text: Recurring job creation failed when the automation's assignee was deactivated
items.desc: If the team member assigned by a “create task” automation was later deactivated, new recurring jobs stopped generating. Fixed.

items.text: Printing the Pending Activation accounts list only showed partial results
items.desc: The print view was capping out around 30–35 rows instead of showing the full list. Fixed.

items.text: Proposals edit page was slow to load
items.desc: Performance fix for the proposal editing page — it loads faster now.

items.text: Mobile app: snack bar buttons disappeared
items.desc: Action buttons in the mobile app's snack-bar notifications had stopped appearing. Fixed.

items.text: Desktop app: shell extension not registering after upgrade
items.desc: After a major desktop app upgrade, the Windows shell extension wasn't re-registering properly, which broke Explorer integration. Fixed.

poll.label: Charging clients' saved payment methods for them
Faster CRM imports with a preview screen
